concept: Seal / Authentication Mark / To Stamp Official
description: A device for stamping an authenticating mark; to close or authenticate. 'Kathu' names what confirms—the mark that proves authenticity or closure.
sound_symbolism: The word stamps with authority. 'Ka' strikes with velar decisiveness, the firm press of seal against surface. Then 'thu' holds with dental permanence, the impression set and enduring. Two syllables, two actions: press and hold. The word feels as definitive as the act it names.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo kathu' (the seal). As verb: 'mi kathu' (I seal). For authentication, closing letters, official marks.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Seals confirmed identity—the personal mark that authenticated documents across distance.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Authenticity as practice—the seal represents integrity, being true to one's word.
